steps.title: Recognize the discriminant condition for one solution
steps.content: A quadratic equation has exactly one solution when its discriminant equals zero. For $-2x^2 + 20x + c = 0$, we have $a = -2$, $b = 20$, and $c$ is unknown.

steps.title: Apply the discriminant formula and solve for c
steps.content:
Substitute the coefficients:
$$20^2 - 4(-2)c = 0$$
$$400 + 8c = 0$$
$$c = -50$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
